Add necessary columns to the table.
The question asks about the name of the first ship to become active. We need to know the activation year of each ship. We extract the value from column "years active" and create a different column "activation year" for each row. The datatype is datetype.

Filter out useless columns.
similar words of the question link to columns:

become active -> status

active -> status
semantic sentence of the question link to columns:

The question is asking to determine the name of the first ship to become active. Since there is a "years active" column that contains date strings, we need to sort the data using the f_sort_column(years active) operation with the order "small to large" (chronological order) to see which ship became active first.

The first ship to become active is determined by looking at the earliest "years active" start date across all rows. 

- Henry J. Kaiser: active from 1986–present
- Joshua Humphreys: active from 1987-1996; 2005-2006; 2010-present
- John Lenthall: active from 1987-1996; 1998–present
- Walter S. Diehl: active from 1988–present
- Leroy Grumman: active from 1989–present
- Pecos: active from 1990–present
- John Ericsson: active from 1991–present
- Kanawha: active from 1991–present
- Guadalupe: active from 1992–present
- Big Horn: active from 1992–present
- Tippecanoe: active from 1993–present
- Yukon: active from 1994–present
- Patuxent: active from 1995–present
- Rappahannock: active from 1995–present
- Laramie: active from 1996–present

The earliest start date among these is 1986 for Henry J. Kaiser. Therefore, Henry J. Kaiser was the first ship to become active.
